# Measuring Success

The project will be considered successful when the following criteria are met:
- a working open-source toolkit capable of collecting and structuring strike data
- reproducible generation of a clean, analysis-ready dataset
- comprehensive documentation enabling new users to run the toolkit independently
- publication of the codebase in a public repository
- evidence of use in academic or analytical workflows, including time series or econometric applications

Additional indicators of success may include community feedback, issue reports, and contributions from external users following release.

# Future Development

The project is intentionally scoped as a foundation for future work.
Potential extensions include:
- expanding geographic coverage and time periods
- integrating additional strike data sources
- evolving the toolkit into a fully packaged R library
- adding analytical utilities for summarisation and visualisation
- linking strike data with broader labour market datasets
- support for panel data structures and extended time series coverage
By delivering a well-documented and extensible core tool, the project creates a foundation for ongoing development and collaboration within the R community.
